name: Aspiration Pneumonia
description: Aspiration pneumonia occurs when foreign material — food, vomit, or liquid — is inhaled into the lungs, causing bacterial infection and inflammation. It can be secondary to megaoesophagus, laryngeal paralysis, or anaesthetic complications.
treatment_overview: Hospitalisation with oxygen therapy, intravenous antibiotics, nebulisation, and coupage (chest physiotherapy) are standard treatments. Underlying causes must be addressed.
prevention_tips: Feed dogs with megaoesophagus in upright position (Bailey chair)
